title: Lively French Chicken Coq Au Vin
description: A buttery, snappy crowd-pleaser that blends classic coq au vin with a low-carb twist, accented by a subtle cream of tartar thickener and a crusty long-fermentation bread.
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C for the crusty bread.
In a large pot, melt 30g butter with 30ml olive oil over medium heat.
Add diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and diced bacon, browning the bacon until crisp.
Add chicken thighs, brown all sides until golden.
Pour in 480ml red wine, bring to a simmer and deglaze the pan.
Add 200g sliced mushrooms, 240ml chicken stock, and 5g salt & pepper; simmer uncovered for 20 minutes.
Stir in 2g cream of tartar, letting the sauce reduce and thicken for an additional 10 minutes.
While the sauce simmers, place a 200g long-fermentation dough on parchment, brush with olive oil, bake for 15 minutes until golden, and serve the chicken topped with sauce alongside the warm bread.
